SEARCH
Package org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.key

Class WorldKeyOrName

java.lang.Object
org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.key.WorldKeyOrName
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<WorldKeyOrName>, LocalizableMessage
Direct Known Subclasses:
WorldKeyOrName.Key, WorldKeyOrName.Name
@AvailableSince("5.7") public abstract sealed class WorldKeyOrName extends Object implements Comparable<WorldKeyOrName>, LocalizableMessage permits WorldKeyOrName.Key, WorldKeyOrName.Name
Represents either a world key or a world name, with methods to parse from strings and retrieve the key or name.
Since:
5.7

    Represent this value as an Either where the left side is the NamespacedKey and the right side is the world name.

    Sorts the instances based on their serialized form, which ensures that keys and names are sorted in an alphabetical manner by default.

  • Constructor Details

    • WorldKeyOrName

      public WorldKeyOrName()

  • Method Details

    • parse

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public static Attempt<WorldKeyOrName,WorldKeyParseFailReason> parse(@Nullable @Nullable String nameOrKey)
      Parse a string into a WorldKeyOrName instance.

      The provided input will be interpreted as a namespaced key if it contains a ':' character, otherwise it will be treated as a world name.

      Parameters:
      nameOrKey - The input string to parse, may be a world name or a namespaced key string
      Returns:
      An Attempt containing either a parsed WorldKeyOrName on success or a WorldKeyParseFailReason describing the failure
      Since:
      5.7

    • parseName

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public static Attempt<WorldKeyOrName,WorldKeyParseFailReason> parseName(@NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Parse a world name into a WorldKeyOrName instance.

      This will attempt to create a usable NamespacedKey from the given name using NamespacedKey.minecraft(String). If parsing fails, a failure Attempt will be returned describing an invalid world name.

      Parameters:
      name - The world name to parse
      Returns:
      An Attempt containing a WorldKeyOrName.Name on success or a WorldKeyParseFailReason.INVALID_WORLD_NAME failure on error
      Since:
      5.7

    • parseKey

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public static Attempt<WorldKeyOrName,WorldKeyParseFailReason> parseKey(@NotNull @NotNull String nameOrKey)
      Parse a namespaced key string into a WorldKeyOrName instance.
      Parameters:
      nameOrKey - The namespaced key string to parse (e.g. "minecraft:world")
      Returns:
      An Attempt containing a WorldKeyOrName.Key on success or a WorldKeyParseFailReason.INVALID_NAMESPACED_KEY failure on error
      Since:
      5.7

    • parseKey

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public static WorldKeyOrName parseKey(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.NamespacedKey key)
      Create a WorldKeyOrName representing a namespaced key.
      Parameters:
      key - The NamespacedKey to wrap
      Returns:
      A WorldKeyOrName containing the provided key
      Since:
      5.7

    • isName

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public abstract boolean isName()
      Returns true if this instance represents a world name (not a namespaced key).
      Returns:
      true if this is a name variant
      Since:
      5.7

    • isKey

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public abstract boolean isKey()
      Returns true if this instance represents a namespaced key (not a world name).
      Returns:
      true if this is a key variant
      Since:
      5.7

    • getKey

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public abstract @NotNull io.vavr.control.Option<org.bukkit.NamespacedKey> getKey()
      Get the underlying NamespacedKey if this instance is a key variant.
      Returns:
      An Option containing the key when present, otherwise Option.none()
      Since:
      5.7

    • getName

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public abstract @NotNull io.vavr.control.Option<String> getName()
      Get the underlying world name if this instance is a name variant.
      Returns:
      An Option containing the name when present, otherwise Option.none()
      Since:
      5.7

    • asEither

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public abstract @NotNull io.vavr.control.Either<org.bukkit.NamespacedKey,String> asEither()
      Represent this value as an Either where the left side is the NamespacedKey and the right side is the world name.
      Returns:
      An Either containing either the key (left) or the name (right)
      Since:
      5.7

    • usableKey

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public abstract @NotNull org.bukkit.NamespacedKey usableKey()
      Get a usable NamespacedKey for this instance.

      If this instance is a key variant, the underlying key is returned. If it is a name variant, an implementation-provided usable key (derived from the name) is returned.

      Returns:
      A non-null NamespacedKey that can be used where a key is required
      Since:
      5.7

    • usableName

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public abstract @NotNull String usableName()
      Get a usable name for this instance.

      If this instance is a name variant, the underlying name is returned. If it is a key variant, a reasonable string representation derived from the key is returned.

      Returns:
      A non-null String representing a usable name
      Since:
      5.7

    • serialise

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public abstract @NotNull String serialise()
      Serialize this instance to a string form suitable for storage or comparison.
      Returns:
      The serialized representation of this value
      Since:
      5.7

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(WorldKeyOrName o)
      Sorts the instances based on their serialized form, which ensures that keys and names are sorted in an alphabetical manner by default.
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<WorldKeyOrName>
      Parameters:
      o - the object to be compared.
      Returns:
      a negative integer, zero, or a positive integer as this object is less than, equal to, or greater than the specified object.

    • getLocalizableMessage

      @Nullable public @Nullable Message getLocalizableMessage()
      Gets the localizable message for this object.
      This can be null if the object does not have a localizable message.
      The returned message will be localized with the PluginLocales provided by the CommandIssuer that called the method which returned this object.
      Specified by:
      getLocalizableMessage in interface LocalizableMessage
      Returns:
      The localizable message, or null if none exists.
